name: Path Traversal: Log Export
description: An ops dashboard exports server logs from /app/logs by filename. The export endpoint joins user input onto that base path with no containment check.

hint_tiers.text: Find the route that streams a file based on a query parameter. What does Path(base) / user_input do when user_input contains ..?

Deployment notes say the app runs at /app and application secrets live at /secrets/super-secret/flag.txt. If the most obvious spelling disappears before the file is opened, what similar-looking input would still normalize to the same path?

Try /export?file=....//....//secrets/super-secret/flag.txt. The filter removes ../ once, leaving a valid traversal. The fix is to resolve the candidate path with .resolve() and assert it stays under the logs base directory.
